channel.title: Server on FantasyMaps
channel.description: Recent content in Server on FantasyMaps

item.title: geomqtt
item.description:
A small daemon that bridges Redis’s geospatial commands and an embedded MQTT broker. Drop tile-keyed coordinates into a Redis GEO set; any client subscribed to the matching tile topic gets the update on the next tick.

It is the transport layer that makes the rest of the shared-world experiments possible — Foundry tables, web clients, and Unity/Unreal sessions can all listen on the same tiles and see each other move.

item.title: georender
item.description:
The symbolic renderer that gives the atlas its look. georender takes geospatial data, runs it through a JSON ruleset, and emits PNGs — as slippy-map tiles, as full bounding-box images, or as ad-hoc renders of arbitrary GeoJSON.

Where the cartographer’s red ink and the dotted park boundaries actually get drawn.

  • Repository
